title: Space/join returns method_ok('error') instead of method_error
description: When an invite code is not found or expired, space_join returns method_ok('error', {...}) instead of method_error(). Per JMAP RFC 8620, a method error response must be ['error', {type, description}, callId]. The current code wraps the error in a success tuple. Strict JMAP clients and any future client checking the method name will misparse this as a successful call to an unknown method named 'error'. The correct pattern used elsewhere in jmap.rs is method_error(call_id, 'notFound', ...).
close_reason: method_ok('error',...) replaced with proper ('error', json!({type: notFound,...})) tuple in space_join
